docs.title: TUI Overall: 565 pass / 11 fail / 576 total — ~70% estimated coverage
Gap #1: store/memories.go — All CRUD operations (5.5% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/store
  • Untested functions: AddMemory, GetMemory, DeleteMemory, CountMemories, ListMemories, UpdateMemory, ContentExists, UpdateTrustFields, IncrementRetrievalCount
  • Why it matters: These are the most-called functions in the entire system. Every memory operation goes through this code. A bug here corrupts all data.
  • Test sketch: TestAddMemory_WithEmbedding — create a PostgresStore with mock pool, call AddMemory with a vector, verify INSERT was called. TestGetMemory_IncludeArchived — verify correct query is used based on includeArchived flag.

docs.title: Gap #2: store/memories.go — Relationship operations (5.5% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/store
  • Untested functions: CreateRelationship, DeleteRelationship, GetRelationships, GetRelationshipSummary, GetSupersessionChain, GetSuperseded
  • Why it matters: The knowledge graph and dialectic engine depend on these. A broken GetRelationships breaks contradiction detection.
  • Test sketch: TestGetRelationships_MalformedRow — insert a row with NULL in a non-nullable column, verify the error is logged (not silently dropped).

docs.title: Gap #3: store/memories.go — Entity operations (5.5% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/store
  • Untested functions: UpsertEntity, GetEntity, GetEntitiesByType, SearchEntities, CreateEntityRelationship, GetEntityRelationships, ResolveEntityGraph, InferenceChain
  • Why it matters: The KG subsystem is a core feature. InferenceChain BFS traversal is complex and untested.
  • Test sketch: TestInferenceChain_ShortestPath — create entities A→B→C, verify BFS finds A→C path.

docs.title: Gap #4: store/memories.go — Peer operations (5.5% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/store
  • Untested functions: AddPeer, GetPeer, ListPeers, UpdatePeerLastActive, ShareMemory, RevokeShareMemory, GetSharedMemories, GetPeerMemories
  • Why it matters: Multi-peer memory sharing is a key differentiator. Untested sharing logic could leak data.
  • Test sketch: TestShareMemory_RoundTrip — share a memory with a peer, verify GetSharedMemories returns it.

docs.title: Gap #5: store/memories.go — Thought chain operations (5.5% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/store
  • Untested functions: StartThoughtChain, AddThought, GetThoughtChain, GetRelatedChains, ReviseThought, BranchThought, PauseThoughtChain, ResumeThoughtChain, AbandonThoughtChain
  • Why it matters: The thought chain system is the core of the Boomerang Protocol. Untested branching/revision logic could lose reasoning context.
  • Test sketch: TestReviseThought_CreatesRevision — add a thought, revise it, verify revision has is_revision=true and revises_thought_id points to original.

docs.title: Gap #6: store/memories.go — Project chunk operations (5.5% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/store
  • Untested functions: AddProjectChunk, DeleteChunksByPath, SearchChunks, GetFileChunksByPath
  • Why it matters: The project indexer depends on these. SearchChunks has dynamic SQL construction with path/file type filters — SQL injection risk.
  • Test sketch: TestSearchChunks_DynamicSQL — search with path and file type filters, verify correct WHERE clause construction.

docs.title: Gap #7: search/hybrid.go — RRF fusion logic (26.8% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: memory/search
  • Untested functions: rrfFuseMultiList, rrfMerge, autoQuery (dual-model RRF), gpuQuery, cpuQuery
  • Why it matters: The RRF fusion is the core of the search quality. A bug here produces wrong search results.
  • Test sketch: TestRRFFuseMultiList_ThreeLists — create 3 ranked lists with overlapping items, verify RRF scores are computed correctly and items are sorted by descending score.

docs.title: Gap #8: context_builder.go — All 5 exported functions (0% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: orchestrator
  • Untested functions: BuildContextPackage, StoreContextPackage, FetchContextPackage, StoreAgentWrapUp, FetchAgentWrapUp
  • Why it matters: This is the core orchestrator logic for building agent context packages. A bug here sends wrong context to agents.
  • Test sketch: TestBuildContextPackage_WithRelevantMemories — create a mock MemoryProvider that returns 3 memories, call BuildContextPackage, verify RelevantFiles and CodeSnippets are populated.

docs.title: Gap #9: broker.go — Call, HealthDeep, MatchIntent (55% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: broker
  • Untested functions: Call (access denied, server not found, no pipes), HealthDeep (context timeout, nil pipes), MatchIntent (no tools available, role filtering)
  • Why it matters: Call is the primary entry point for tool dispatch. A bug here blocks all MCP tool calls.
  • Test sketch: TestBroker_Call_AccessDenied — call a server the role doesn't have access to, verify ErrUnauthorized with available server hints.

docs.title: Gap #10: backend-go/main.go — JSON-RPC dispatch (32.3% coverage)
docs.text:
• Package: backend/cmd
  • Untested functions: JSON-RPC method dispatch, error handling, signal handling, JSONBrokerCallResult (nil Result edge case)
  • Why it matters: This is the entry point for all TUI-to-backend communication. A bug here breaks the entire system.
  • Test sketch: TestJSONBrokerCallResult_NilResult — call a tool that returns nil result, verify the response is handled correctly.

docs.text:
1. TestBroker_DeregisterServer_DoesNotStopOtherProxies — broker_integration_test.go
  2. Register two servers, start both, deregister one, verify the other can still receive calls.

  3. Catches: Bug #3 (CRITICAL) — proxy sharing.

  4. TestBroker_Call_ContextCancellation — broker_api_test.go

  7. TestCountMemories_PrimaryQuery — store/memories_test.go

  14. Create mock MemoryProvider, call BuildContextPackage, verify output structure.
  15. Catches: Zero test coverage on core orchestrator logic.

1. TestRRFFuseMultiList_ThreeLists — search/hybrid_test.go
  2. Create 3 ranked lists with overlapping items, verify RRF scores.

  3. Catches: RRF fusion correctness.

  4. TestInferenceChain_ShortestPath — store/memories_test.go

  2. TestNeuralgenticsClient_MockSubprocess — neuralgentics-client.test.ts

Report complete. 4 packages with critical coverage gaps (<30%), 10 specific gaps identified, 12 recommended tests in priority order.

docs.title: Session 23 — Reviewer Report (T-062)
docs.text: Date: 2026-06-05 Reviewer: neuralgentics-tester (deepseek-v4-flash) Baseline: neuralgentics v0.1.0, 131 .go files (60 *_test.go), 54 .ts source files + 21 test files Architect Input: docs/design/session-23-architect-review.md (585 lines)

docs.title: #1 (CRITICAL) — Broker.DeregisterServer kills shared proxy for ALL servers
docs.text:
• File: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/broker.go:206-216
  • Problem: DeregisterServer calls b.proxy.Stop() which closes the single shared MCPProxy's reader channel and all pending response channels. Since NewBroker() creates exactly one MCPProxy (line 54), stopping it kills the async reader for every server, not just the one being deregistered. ReloadServer (line 146) and ReloadServerWithConfig (line 188) have the same issue.
  • Fix: Per-server proxy tracking. Each StartServer should create a dedicated reader goroutine; DeregisterServer stops only that server's reader. Alternatively, use a reference-counted reader that only stops when the last server deregisters.
  • Test: Add TestBroker_DeregisterServer_DoesNotStopOtherProxies — register two servers, start both, deregister one, verify the other can still receive calls.

docs.title: #2 (CRITICAL) — Broker reload test hangs indefinitely (30s timeout)
docs.text:
• File: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/reload_test.go:102
  • Problem: TestReloadServer_RunningServer hangs for 30 seconds and times out. Stack trace shows goroutine stuck in proxy.go:104 (readLoop scanner). The test starts a real subprocess (e.g., cat or sleep), and when the process exits, the scanner blocks on stdout.Read() because the pipe is not closed. The Stop() method closes readerCh but the scanner is blocked on Read(), not on the channel select.
  • Fix: The readLoop at proxy.go:100 uses a non-blocking select on readerCh (line 106-110) but the scanner.Scan() at line 104 blocks the goroutine. When the subprocess exits, scanner.Scan() returns false (EOF), but if the pipe is never closed (e.g., subprocess hangs), the scanner blocks forever. Fix: use io.Copy with a context or set a read deadline on the pipe. Alternatively, close the stdout pipe explicitly in Stop().
  • Test: Already exists — just needs the fix to pass.

docs.title: #3 (HIGH) — GetMemory passes includeArchived bool as SQL parameter incorrectly
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories.go:94-99
  • Problem: When includeArchived=false, the code passes includeArchived (a bool) as the second query parameter to GetMemoryByID. The query at queries.go:67 is WHERE id = $1 AND ($2::boolean OR is_archived = FALSE). When $2=false, this becomes WHERE id = $1 AND (false OR is_archived = FALSE) which is correct. However, when includeArchived=true, the code uses GetMemoryByIDIncludeArchived (no $2). This is actually correct — the code path is correct for both cases. RETRACTED — the architect flagged this but it's not a bug. The code correctly branches on includeArchived.
  • Verdict: FALSE ALARM — code is correct.

docs.title: #4 (HIGH) — Silent error swallowing in 7+ Scan loops
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories.go:272,314,381,404,439,1452,1479
  • Problem: Row scanning errors use continue instead of logging or returning. Malformed database rows are silently dropped, making debugging impossible. Affected methods: GetRelationships, GetSupersessionChain, GetEntitiesByType, SearchEntities, GetEntityRelationships, SearchChunks, GetFileChunksByPath.
  • Fix: Replace continue with at minimum slog.Warn("scan error", "err", err) before continue. For critical paths, return the error.
  • Test: Add TestGetRelationships_MalformedRow — insert a row with NULL in a non-nullable column, verify the error is logged (not silently dropped).

docs.title: #5 (HIGH) — store package has 5.5% test coverage
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/ (memories.go, postgres.go, queries.go, search.go, agent_tools.go)
  • Problem: The store package is the largest and most critical package (1,768-line god-file + 378-line postgres.go + 653-line queries.go) but has only 5.5% statement coverage. The search package is similarly low at 26.8%. These packages contain all database CRUD operations, relationships, entities, peers, thought chains, audit, trust, project chunks, and 1024-dim operations.
  • Fix: Add unit tests with mock pgx.Rows and pgx.Row interfaces. At minimum, test AddMemory, GetMemory, CountMemories, CreateRelationship, UpsertEntity, AddPeer, StartThoughtChain, LogAuditEvent, AddProjectChunk, SearchChunks.
  • Test: See PART B — Coverage Audit for specific test recommendations.

docs.title: #6 (HIGH) — context_builder.go has zero tests
docs.text:
• File: packages/orchestrator-go/src/neuralgentics/orchestrator/context_builder.go (495 lines)
  • Problem: BuildContextPackage, StoreContextPackage, FetchContextPackage, StoreAgentWrapUp, FetchAgentWrapUp — 5 exported functions, 0 tests. This is the core orchestrator logic for building agent context packages.
  • Fix: Add context_builder_test.go with mock MemoryProvider. Test BuildContextPackage with various task types, test StoreContextPackage round-trip, test error paths (memory query failure, marshal failure).
  • Test: TestBuildContextPackage_WithRelevantMemories, TestStoreContextPackage_RoundTrip, TestBuildContextPackage_EmptyMemories.

docs.title: #7 (HIGH) — Broker Call has no timeout
docs.text:
• File: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/broker.go:299-330
  • Problem: Call method has no context or timeout parameter. A hung MCP server blocks the broker indefinitely. The underlying proxy.sendRPC has a 30-second timeout (proxy.go:339), but the broker's Call doesn't expose this to callers or allow cancellation.
  • Fix: Add ctx context.Context parameter to Call. Use select with ctx.Done() in sendRPC. This is a breaking API change but necessary for production reliability.
  • Test: TestBroker_Call_ContextCancellation — cancel context before call completes, verify error.

docs.title: #8 (MEDIUM) — CountMemories broken primary query
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories.go:146
  • Problem: QueryRow(ctx, GetMemoryCount).Scan(nil, &active, nil) — scans into nil for two of three columns. The primary query GetMemoryCount returns 3 columns but only 1 is scanned. The fallback raw SQL at line 149 works correctly.
  • Fix: Fix GetMemoryCount SQL to return only 1 column, or fix the Scan to match the query columns.
  • Test: TestCountMemories_PrimaryQuery — verify the primary query path works (not just the fallback).

docs.title: #9 (MEDIUM) — Stub methods return nil,nil (indistinguishable from "no results")
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories.go:1302-1304 (GetTrustAdjustments), 1320-1322 (ListFadingMemories)
  • Problem: Both methods return nil, nil with // TODO comments. Callers cannot distinguish "no results" from "not implemented". The ListFadingMemories signature includes threshold float64 and limit int parameters that are completely ignored.
  • Fix: Either implement the queries or return fmt.Errorf("not implemented").
  • Test: TestGetTrustAdjustments_NotImplemented — verify error is returned, not nil.

docs.title: #10 (MEDIUM) — Security summary uses string-cast hours parameter
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories.go:1705
  • Problem: hoursStr := fmt.Sprintf("%d", hours) converts the integer to a string and passes it as a SQL argument. The queries use $1::integer cast, so PostgreSQL will cast it back, but this is fragile and non-idiomatic. Should pass hours directly as int.
  • Fix: Pass hours as int directly. The SQL queries use $1::integer which accepts both int and string parameters, but passing a string is misleading.
  • Test: TestGetSecuritySummary_HoursParameter — verify the query executes with integer parameter.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories.go (1,768 LOC)
docs.text: God-file with 61 methods spanning 10+ domains. Contains memory CRUD, relationships, entities, peers, thought chains, audit, trust/decay, project chunks, 1024-dim ops, user profiles, and security summaries. 7 silent error swallowing sites (rows.Scan errors continued). 2 stub methods returning nil,nil. 1 broken primary query (CountMemories). 1 non-idiomatic SQL parameter (hoursStr). The GetMemory branching logic is correct (verified). 5.5% coverage — the most critical coverage gap in the entire codebase.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/memory.go (670 LOC)
docs.text: Clean facade pattern. Well-structured with clear section headers. Dual-write 1024-dim sidecar in AddMemory is best-effort with proper logging. IndexProject background goroutine correctly uses jobsMu mutex. No bugs found. Coverage: ~85% (via integration tests).

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/postgres.go (378 LOC)
docs.text: Clean initialization with production-tuned pool settings (MinConns=4, MaxConns=25). Migration runner uses embedded FS. scanMemoryEntry and scanMemoryEntryWithDistance are well-structured. parseVectorString is correct. No dedicated tests — covered only via integration tests.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/queries.go (653 LOC)
docs.text: SQL constants only. No logic to test. Well-organized with clear section headers. One concern: GetMemoryByID at line 67 uses $2::boolean OR is_archived = FALSE — the $2 parameter is the includeArchived bool. When false, the query correctly filters to non-archived. When true, the code uses GetMemoryByIDIncludeArchived instead. Correct.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/index/indexer.go (248 LOC)
docs.text: Clean implementation with atomic.Bool for concurrent indexing protection. Batch processing with configurable batch size. Context cancellation respected. The addChunkWithVector method at line 197-206 is a workaround for ChunkResult not having a Vector field — the vector is silently dropped. Coverage: 85.9% (but the concurrent indexing test fails).

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/index/indexer_test.go (646 LOC)
docs.text: Mock store with 20+ stub methods for interface compliance. The TestProjectIndexer_Index_ConcurrentIndexing test at line 537 fails because the mock is too fast — the first indexing completes before the 50ms sleep finishes. The atomic.Bool works correctly; the test timing is wrong.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/search/hybrid.go (331 LOC)
docs.text: Well-implemented dual-model RRF search. Three modes (cpu/auto/gpu) with proper fallback. rrfFuseMultiList correctly handles dedup and sorting. HybridSearch has proper error handling (if one search fails, returns the other). Coverage: 26.8% — the RRF fusion logic is untested.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/dialectic/engine.go (350 LOC)
docs.text: Clean implementation. FindContradictions does explicit CONTRADICTS relationship search + implicit text similarity. Proper dedup with seen map. Best-effort implicit search (errors don't fail the whole operation). Coverage: 85.0%.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/trust/engine.go (126 LOC)
docs.text: Small, focused, well-tested. AdjustTrust correctly clamps to [0.0, 1.0], updates store, and logs adjustment. Coverage: 80.0%.

docs.title: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/kg/queries.go (335 LOC)
docs.text: BFS-based inference chain finding with cycle detection. Query dispatches to findInferenceChain or transitiveClosure based on whether EndEntity is set. Coverage: 78.6%.

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/broker.go (427 LOC)
docs.text: Clean facade with 20+ methods. Critical bug: DeregisterServer (line 210), ReloadServer (line 146), ReloadServerWithConfig (line 188) all call b.proxy.Stop() which kills the shared proxy for ALL servers. High: Call has no context/timeout parameter. Medium: MatchIntent creates a new IntentMatcher on every call (line 353). Coverage: ~55% (main broker package).

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/proxy/proxy.go (345 LOC)
docs.text: Async reader goroutine with channel-based response routing. sendRPC has 30s timeout. Stop() correctly closes all pending channels. Bug: readLoop at line 104 blocks on scanner.Scan() — the non-blocking select on readerCh at line 106-110 only works between scan iterations, not during a blocking read. If the subprocess hangs, the scanner blocks forever. Coverage: 78.8%.

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/access/access.go (271 LOC)
docs.text: Clean access control with role-based permissions. Coverage: 100%.

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/catalog/catalog.go (198 LOC)
docs.text: Server catalog builder with role filtering. Coverage: 83.9%.

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/launcher/launcher.go (195 LOC)
docs.text: Subprocess lifecycle management. Coverage: 67.2%.

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/intent/matcher.go (279 LOC)
docs.text: Jaccard similarity intent matcher. Coverage: 96.4%.

docs.title: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/registry/registry.go (155 LOC)
docs.text: In-memory server registry with RWMutex. Coverage: 38.5% — low for a simple data structure.

docs.title: packages/orchestrator-go/src/neuralgentics/orchestrator/task_runner.go (638 LOC)
docs.text: Kahn's algorithm topological sort, parallel group computation, ready/blocked task detection. nowMillis returns hardcoded 0 (line 583-585). tasksToEntries hardcodes AgentCoder (line 592) even though dispatchGroup later calls ResolveAgent. Coverage: ~55% (via orchestrator package).

docs.title: packages/orchestrator-go/src/neuralgentics/orchestrator/context_builder.go (495 LOC)
docs.text: 5 exported functions, 0 tests. BuildContextPackage queries memories, builds trust scores, extracts decisions/files/snippets, resolves scope/output/error handling. StoreContextPackage marshals to JSON and stores in memory. FetchContextPackage round-trips. All untested.

docs.title: packages/orchestrator-go/src/neuralgentics/orchestrator/protocol.go (266 LOC)
docs.text: 8-step Boomerang Protocol state machine with RWMutex. Advance validates transitions. Enforce blocks in strict mode. CompleteProtocol advances through remaining steps. Well-implemented. No dedicated tests (covered by e2e tests which fail due to Docker unavailability).

docs.title: packages/orchestrator-go/src/neuralgentics/orchestrator/orchestrator.go (371 LOC)
docs.text: Top-level orchestrator with HandleTask, Route, Dispatch, CompleteTaskCycle. Uses mutex for state. Coverage: ~55% (via orchestrator package).

docs.title: packages/backend-go/cmd/backend/main.go (2,166 LOC)
docs.text: Thin JSON-RPC stdio server. Large but mostly boilerplate (JSON-RPC dispatch, type conversions). Coverage: 32.3% (backend package) / 100% (types package).

docs.title: packages/tui/src/__tests__/neuralgentics-client.test.ts (259 LOC)
docs.text: 11 tests fail — all in the NeuralgenticsClient unit test suite. The tests mock a subprocess but the neuralgentics-backend binary is either not available or not behaving as expected. The resolveBackendPath tests also fail because the binary path resolution depends on the test environment.

docs.title: Bug #1: TestProjectIndexer_Index_ConcurrentIndexing — CONFIRMED
docs.text:
• File: packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/index/indexer_test.go:537
  • Status: FAILS 3/3 runs
  • Root cause: The test starts indexing in a goroutine, sleeps 50ms, then tries a second concurrent call. The mock store is too fast (simple map insert with mutex) — the first indexing completes before the 50ms sleep. The atomic.Bool in indexer.go:22-23 works correctly; the test timing is wrong.
  • Fix: Add a synchronization point in the mock (e.g., a channel that blocks AddProjectChunk until the test signals readiness).
  • Adjacent issues: The mock store at indexer_test.go:18-24 has 20+ stub methods for interface compliance. These stubs (lines 622-646) implement GetUserProfile, UpsertUserProfile, GetSecuritySummary, RecordToolRequest, IncrementToolUse, GetAgentTools — all returning nil/nil. This is the same pattern as the production stubs.

docs.title: Bug #2: TUI test failures — CONFIRMED (11 failures)
docs.text:
• File: packages/tui/src/__tests__/neuralgentics-client.test.ts
  • Status: 11 tests fail consistently
  • Root cause: The NeuralgenticsClient tests spawn a subprocess and communicate via JSON-RPC over stdio. The tests fail because the subprocess (neuralgentics-backend) either doesn't exist in the test PATH or doesn't produce the expected JSON output. The resolveBackendPath tests fail because the binary path resolution depends on environment variables that may not be set in CI.
  • Fix: Use a mock subprocess (e.g., a script that echoes valid JSON-RPC responses) instead of the real binary. Or set NEURALGENTICS_BACKEND_PATH in the test environment.
  • Adjacent issues: setup.test.ts has 5 tests that all pass. The resolveBackendPath tests in neuralgentics-client.test.ts are the ones failing (2 of the 11).

docs.title: Bug #3: Broker.DeregisterServer stops all proxies — CONFIRMED
docs.text:
• File: packages/broker-go/src/neuralgentics/broker/broker.go:206-216
  • Status: Verified by reading proxy.go:Stop() at line 167-182
  • Root cause: NewBroker() at line 54 creates a single MCPProxy instance. DeregisterServer at line 210 calls b.proxy.Stop() which closes p.readerCh (signals reader goroutine to exit) and closes all pending channels. After this, no RPC calls can be made through the proxy. ReloadServer (line 146) and ReloadServerWithConfig (line 188) have the same issue.
  • Fix: Per-server reader tracking. Each StartServer should create a dedicated reader goroutine scoped to that server's stdout pipe. DeregisterServer stops only that server's reader.
  • Adjacent issues: The TestReloadServer_RunningServer test at reload_test.go:102 hangs for 30 seconds and times out. Stack trace shows goroutine stuck in proxy.go:104 (readLoop scanner). This is a separate but related issue — the scanner blocks on stdout.Read() even after Stop() is called because the non-blocking select on readerCh only works between scan iterations.

docs.title: Fix #1: Fix Broker reload test hang (HIGH) — ~1 hour
docs.text:
• Dependency: None
  • Scope: proxy/proxy.go:100-155 — make readLoop interruptible during blocking reads
  • Approach: Use io.Copy with a context or set a read deadline on the pipe. When Stop() is called, close the stdout pipe to unblock the scanner.
  • Why first: This is blocking CI. The 30s timeout wastes developer time.

docs.title: Fix #2: Fix Broker.DeregisterServer proxy sharing (CRITICAL) — ~2 hours
docs.text:
• Dependency: Fix #1 (need working proxy before refactoring)
  • Scope: broker.go, proxy/proxy.go — per-server reader tracking
  • Approach: Add StartReader(name string, stdout io.Reader) and StopReader(name string) to proxy. Track readers in a map. DeregisterServer calls StopReader(name) instead of Stop().
  • Why second: This is a correctness bug that silently breaks multi-server deployments.

docs.title: Fix #3: Fix silent error swallowing in Scan loops (HIGH) — ~15 minutes
docs.text:
• Dependency: None
  • Scope: 7 sites in store/memories.go
  • Approach: Replace continue with slog.Warn("scan error", "err", err); continue
  • Why third: Quick win with immediate debugging improvement.

docs.title: Fix #4: Fix CountMemories broken primary query (MEDIUM) — ~15 minutes
docs.text:
• Dependency: None
  • Scope: store/memories.go:146
  • Approach: Fix GetMemoryCount SQL to return 1 column, or fix Scan to match 3 columns.
  • Why fourth: Quick correctness fix.

docs.title: Fix #5: Remove or implement stub methods (MEDIUM) — ~15 minutes
docs.text:
• Dependency: None
  • Scope: store/memories.go:1302-1304, 1320-1322
  • Approach: Return fmt.Errorf("not implemented") instead of nil, nil.
  • Why fifth: Prevents silent failures in production.

Report complete. 12 critical/high findings, 3 bug verifications, 5 recommended fixes in dependency order.

docs.title: Session 26 — Coverage Push: God-File Split + Untested Files + RRF Gap
docs.text: Date: 2026-06-05 Phase: Coverage Stabilization Theme: De-risk the 5.5%-coverage store/ package by splitting the 1,773-line god-file Total Estimated Effort: ~6 days (11 cards, 0.5–1 day each) Baseline: neuralgentics v0.1.0, commit e74b7f2, working tree clean

docs.title: Background
docs.text: The store/ package is the data backbone of neuralgentics — every memory operation, relationship, entity, peer, thought chain, audit event, project chunk, and security query goes through PostgresStore. Yet the package has 5.5% test coverage (Session 23 audit, docs/design/session-23-coverage-audit.md), and its heart is a 1,773-line god-file (memories.go) with 78 methods spanning 10+ domains. This is the #1 priority from the Session 23 architect review (docs/design/session-23-architect-review.md, §3, Ranked Refactor Backlog #1).

docs.title: Why Now
docs.text: Metric Current Target store/memories.go LOC 1,773 ~230 after splits (CRUD section only) store/ package coverage 5.5% ≥80% God-file domains in one file 10 1 (CRUD naturally remains) Untested store/ files with dedicated tests 2 (vector.go + partial memories) 6+ (all source files)

docs.title: Safety Property
docs.text: The PostgresStore struct is defined in postgres.go:24. Go allows methods on a type to be defined in any file in the same package. Every extraction is pure code movement — zero logic changes, zero signature changes, zero import changes (all files stay in package store). The only risk is merge conflicts if two extractions modify overlapping line ranges in memories.go simultaneously. See §Parallelization for the safe strategy.

docs.title: Caller Graph (verified grep)
docs.text: All callers access methods via m.store.MethodName() (through the memory.go facade) or s.store.MethodName() (in search/hybrid.go). No caller imports individual files; all access is through the *PostgresStore receiver. Splitting methods into separate files preserves the receiver and method names — zero caller changes needed.

docs.title: T-COV-001: Extract memories_1024.go — 1024-Dim Dual-Model Ops
docs.text: Field Value Files in scope store/memories.go → new store/memories_1024.go Lines to extract Lines 1529–1621 (93 lines): AddMemory1024, QueryMemories1024, GetMemory1024, CountMemories1024, DeleteMemory1024 Target file packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/memories_1024.go Test acceptance ≥5 new unit tests in store/memories_1024_test.go. Tests: TestAddMemory1024_Success, TestAddMemory1024_MissingMemory, TestQueryMemories1024_VectorSearch, TestCountMemories1024_Empty, TestDeleteMemory1024_Success. ≥80% coverage on extracted code. Depends on None (extracts from the bottom of the file — no dependency on any other extraction) Blocks Nothing Effort 0.5 day Risk LOW — self-contained at end of file, no internal cross-references from above Verification go build ./packages/memory/... && go test ./packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/ -run "1024" -count=1

docs.title: T-COV-002: Extract profiles_security.go — User Profiles + Security Summary
docs.text: Field Value Files in scope store/memories.go → new store/profiles_security.go Lines to extract Lines 1622–1773 (152 lines): GetUserProfile, UpsertUserProfile, GetSecuritySummary Target file packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/profiles_security.go Test acceptance ≥4 new unit tests in store/profiles_security_test.go. Tests: TestGetUserProfile_Existing, TestGetUserProfile_NotFound, TestUpsertUserProfile_Create, TestUpsertUserProfile_Update, TestGetSecuritySummary_ValidRange. ≥80% coverage on extracted code. Depends on T-COV-001 (extracts from bottom; depends on 1024 lines being removed first so line numbers are stable) Blocks T-COV-003 (next extraction upward) Effort 0.5 day Risk LOW — self-contained domain at end of file Verification go build ./packages/memory/... && go test ./packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/ -run "Profile|SecuritySummary" -count=1

docs.title: T-COV-003: Extract project_chunks.go — Project Indexer Operations
docs.text: Field Value Files in scope store/memories.go → new store/project_chunks.go Lines to extract Lines 1328–1527 (200 lines): AddProjectChunk, DeleteChunksByPath, SearchChunks, GetFileChunksByPath, plus the formatVector helper (line 1512) Target file packages/memory/src/neuralgentics/memory/store/project_chunks.go Test acceptance ≥5 new unit tests in store/project_chunks_test.go. Tests: TestAddProjectChunk_Success, TestAddProjectChunk_NoPool, TestSearchChunks_DynamicSQL, TestSearchChunks_NoResults, TestGetFileChunksByPath_Reconstruct, TestDeleteChunksByPath_Success. ≥80% coverage on extracted code. Depends on T-COV-002 (1024+profiles extracted, project_chunks now at lines ~1328-end) Blocks T-COV-004 Effort 0.5 day Risk LOW-MEDIUM — SearchChunks has dynamic SQL construction (Session 23 architect smell #4: integer-to-string cast at line 1705); test card should verify no SQL injection. formatVector is shared with 1024 ops — if T-COV-001 already exported it, deduplicate; otherwise copy. docs.title: Dependency Graph
docs.text:
T-COV-001 (1024 ops, bottom)
  └── T-COV-002 (profiles+security, above 1024)
        └── T-COV-003 (project chunks, above profiles)
              └── T-COV-004 (audit+trust, above chunks)
                    └── T-COV-005 (thoughts, above audit) [1 day]
                          └── T-COV-006 (peers, above thoughts)
                                └── T-COV-007 (entities, above peers)
                                      └── T-COV-008 (relationships, top section)
                                            ├── T-COV-009 (test postgres + agent_tools)
                                            ├── T-COV-010 (test search.go)
                                            └── T-COV-011 (RRF coverage)

Why sequential for T-COV-001 through T-COV-008: Each extraction removes lines from memories.go and adds them to a new file. Removing lines shifts all line numbers above. Extracting bottom-first means each card's line references in memories.go remain stable. Parallel extraction of non-overlapping regions is possible (see §Parallelization) but adds merge-conflict risk.

T-COV-009, T-COV-010, T-COV-011 are parallelizable — they touch different files, no shared state.

docs.title: Batch 1: Bottom + Top (2 parallel extractions, if safe)
docs.text:
Card Region Lines Rationale T-COV-001 Bottom (1529–1621) 93 Extracts from end of file; removing these doesn't shift anything above T-COV-008 Top (230–335) 106 Extracts from upper-middle; doesn't overlap with bottom region

Constraint: After Batch 1, memories.go has two "holes" — lines 230–335 and 1529–1621 are removed. Remaining contiguous regions: 1–229 (CRUD), 336–1528 (entities through project chunks), 1622–1773 (profiles). The line numbers for the middle region shift by -106 (relationship extraction above them). This is manageable but requires the Batch 1 coder to document the new line offsets.

Risk with Batch 1 parallel: If T-COV-001 and T-COV-008 both modify memories.go in parallel, git may not merge cleanly (both remove lines from the same file). Recommendation: Run them sequentially unless both coders coordinate through a shared intermediate commit.

docs.title: Batch 2: Sequential Middle Sweep (6 sequential extractions)
docs.text: After Batch 1, extract remaining domains bottom-to-top: 1. T-COV-002 (profiles — now at ~1622-end of remaining file) 2. T-COV-003 (project chunks) 3. T-COV-004 (audit+trust) 4. T-COV-005 (thoughts — largest, 1 day) 5. T-COV-006 (peers) 6. T-COV-007 (entities)

docs.title: Batch 3: Parallel Test Cards (3 parallel)
docs.text: After all extractions, all 3 test cards can run simultaneously: - T-COV-009 (postgres + agent_tools tests) - T-COV-010 (search.go tests) - T-COV-011 (RRF coverage in hybrid.go)

docs.title: Risk Register
docs.text: # Risk Severity Mitigation 1 Merge conflicts on memories.go from parallel extractions MEDIUM Use sequential extraction (bottom-to-top) or coordinate through shared intermediate commits. Each card commits its extraction before the next card starts. 2 Helper functions (scanThoughtRow, derefString, formatVector) lose accessibility LOW All helpers are package-level (unexported, lowercase first letter). They remain accessible from any file in package store. No change needed. If formatVector is needed by both project_chunks.go and memories_1024.go, move it to a shared store/vector_util.go. 3 InferenceChain BFS traversal breaks on extraction LOW Pure code movement — BFS algorithm doesn't change. However, the existing code was untested before extraction; any pre-existing BFS bug becomes visible when tests are added. 4 GetTrustAdjustments + ListFadingMemories stub methods LOW These already return fmt.Errorf("not implemented"). The Session 23 fix (replacing nil,nil with error returns) is already applied. Tests should assert the error is returned. 5 CountMemories broken primary query LOW Session 23 architect smell S5: QueryRow(ctx, GetMemoryCount).Scan(nil, &active, nil) scans into nil columns. The fallback raw SQL works. This is a pre-existing bug, not introduced by extraction. Fix inline during T-COV-008 or add a follow-up card. 6 Silent error swallowing in 7+ scan loops MEDIUM Session 23 architect smell S2: if err := rows.Scan(...); err != nil { continue } in relationships.go, entities.go, peers.go, project_chunks.go. The extraction cards do NOT change logic, so this smell persists. Covered by T-COV-008 acceptance test TestGetRelationships_MalformedRow — but the fix itself (changing continue to error propagation) should be in a separate bugfix card. 7 Testcontainers dependency for DB-backed tests MEDIUM Many tests (search.go, postgres.go, thought chain tests) need a pgx pool. The project uses testcontainers-go; ensure CI has Docker available. Alternatively, use mockpgx for lighter tests. 8 Existing test fixtures (memories_count_test.go, memories_get_test.go) reference PostgresStore methods LOW These test files already import store and call methods directly. After extraction, the methods are still on PostgresStore and still in package store. No fixture changes needed.

docs.title: Roadmap: Neuralgentics v0.1.3 — P2 Phase (Continuity + Offline)
docs.text:
Author: boomerang-architect (deepseek-v4-pro:cloud) Date: 2026-06-05 Status: v0.1.3 P2 (5.5 days) — DESIGN PHASE ONLY Builds on: P0 (13 cards, all done) + P1 (9 cards, all done) + Session 25/26 cleanup

P2 delivers cross-session continuity, offline mode, and panel upgrades. When P2 ships, the TUI survives restarts: session state is checkpointed to neuralgentics, token spend persists, model preferences stick, opportunity findings are cached, and the TUI is usable with minimal functionality even when the OpenCode server or Go backend is down. The diff panel graduates from 2-way to 3-way merge.

Correction from original P0/P1 roadmap: The P0 roadmap (v0.1.0) listed P2 as "Slash Commands + Cross-Session" (~4 days) with agent preferences, multi-session continuity, heartbeat + stale detection, event synthesis, and HANDOFF.md auto-generation. That was inaccurate — slash commands shipped in P1 (T-039, 10 functional), so P2 focuses on persistence, offline resilience, and viewer upgrades. Heartbeat/stale detection and HANDOFF.md auto-generation are deferred to P3 (hardening). Agent preferences are replaced by model-selection persistence (T-082) since the model registry (T-025) already handles task-scoped routing.

docs.title: Top-Level Summary
docs.text:
P0 gave us a working pipeline (TUI + compaction + dispatch + verify). P1 gave us polish (themes, slash commands, token accounting, opportunity detection, kanban with circuit breaker, aggregator-aware lookup, permission gating). Both phases are fully shipped with 650 tests passing.

The gap: nothing survives a TUI restart. The token counter resets to zero. The opportunity detector's 8-pattern findings evaporate. The model choice reverts to defaults. The compaction checkpoints (gemma4:31b's extracted facts + reseed snapshot) are lost. The diff panel can only show 2-way changes. And if the Go backend or OpenCode server goes down, the TUI is a dead terminal — no read-only fallback.

P2 closes these gaps with 7 cards. The foundation is checkpoint persistence (T-079) — serialize compaction cycle output to neuralgentics. On top of that, session resume (T-080) restores the TUI to its previous state on restart, offline mode (T-081) provides read-only degraded operation, model-selection persistence (T-082) remembers the sticky per-session model choice, spend history (T-084) and offline opportunity cache (T-085) persist their data across sessions, and 3-way merge (T-083) upgrades the diff panel.

Note on "/spend & /opportunities go from stub to live": Per the card spec (T-ARCH-P2), this was listed as a P2 theme. However, these panels are already live — T-033 wired TokenCounter to SpendPanel, T-034 wired OpportunityDetector to the /opportunities command, and T-039 made both functional. P2 adds persistence to both (T-084 and T-085) so their data survives restarts. The P2 cards are correctly scoped to persistence, not initial implementation.

docs.title: Task T-079: Checkpoint Persistence
docs.text:
• Goal: Persist compaction cycle output (extracted facts, reseed snapshot, token state, session metadata) as type: "compaction_checkpoint" memories in neuralgentics. On TUI restart, load the most recent checkpoint to restore session state. This is the foundation card for all persistence features (T-080, T-081, T-082, T-084, T-085).
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/compaction/writer.ts — extend CompactionWriter.writeCheckpoint() to serialize a CompactionCheckpoint struct (facts extracted, facts count, tokens before/after, timing, session ID, timestamp, confidence scores) into a JSON blob and call neuralgenticsClient.memory.add() with type: "compaction_checkpoint".
  • packages/tui/src/compaction/types.ts — add CompactionCheckpoint interface: {checkpointId: string, sessionId: string, timestamp: string, factsExtracted: number, tokensBefore: number, tokensAfter: number, savingsRatio: number, reverted: boolean, reseeded: boolean, confidenceScores: Map<string, number>, extractedMemoryIds: string[]}.
  • packages/tui/src/compaction/orchestrator.ts — extend CompactionOrchestrator.compact() to call writer.writeCheckpoint() after successful extraction + writing. Return checkpoint ID in CompactionResult.
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — add loadLastCheckpoint(): Promise<CompactionCheckpoint | null> method that queries neuralgentics for most recent type: "compaction_checkpoint" memory (sorted by timestamp DESC, limit 1). Add restoreFromCheckpoint(checkpoint: CompactionCheckpoint): Promise<void> that repopulates session state.
  • packages/tui/src/neuralgentics-client/client.ts — verify memory.query supports filter: {type: "compaction_checkpoint"} and sort: DESC (the Go backend's QueryMemories method supports sourceType filtering; verify or add this filter path).
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/checkpoint-persistence.test.ts — unit tests: write checkpoint, read back, verify all fields round-trip, handle missing checkpoint gracefully, handle corrupted checkpoint (bad JSON), verify session manager loads most recent.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Restoring the FULL TUI state from checkpoint (→ T-080 wires all the panels to checkpoint restore)
  • Persisting token counter state (→ T-084 owns its own persistence logic)
  • Persisting opportunity detector state (→ T-085 owns its own persistence logic)
  • Persisting model preferences (→ T-082 owns its own persistence logic)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/compaction/types.ts — add CompactionCheckpoint interface (~30 lines)
  • packages/tui/src/compaction/writer.ts — extend with writeCheckpoint(), call neuralgenticsClient.memory.add() (~40 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/compaction/orchestrator.ts — call writer.writeCheckpoint() after writer.writeFacts(), add checkpoint ID to CompactionResult (~20 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — add loadLastCheckpoint() and restoreFromCheckpoint() (~60 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/neuralgentics-client/client.ts — verify/ensure memory.query can filter by sourceType + sort by timestamp (investigate + ~10 lines if needed)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/checkpoint-persistence.test.ts — 12 tests (write, read, round-trip, missing, corrupted, most-recent, empty checkpoint, concurrent checkpoints, restore from checkpoint, invalid JSON, edge cases)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • After compaction cycle completes, a type: "compaction_checkpoint" memory exists in neuralgentics with all fields populated correctly
  • SessionManager.loadLastCheckpoint() returns the most recently created checkpoint
  • Checkpoint includes extracted memory IDs (so T-080 can reload them on resume)
  • No checkpoint exists → loadLastCheckpoint() returns null gracefully (no crash)
  • Corrupted checkpoint JSON → logged as warning, treated as "no checkpoint" (graceful degradation)
  • Checkpoint does NOT duplicate existing extracted facts — each fact is stored once; checkpoint just references their memory IDs
  • bun test: 12 new tests pass; 0 regressions on existing 650
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • All 4 Go modules build + test clean
  • Dependencies: T-026 (compaction) and T-027 (session manager) — both DONE. No new P2 dependencies.
  • Effort: 1.0 day
  • Parallelization: Can run in parallel with T-083 (zero shared files). Blocks T-080, T-081, T-082, T-084, T-085 (all need checkpoint persistence to exist). Coder-only — no architect needed; the schema is straightforward.
  • Risk: LOW — extending existing compaction writer + session manager. Schema is self-contained.

docs.title: Task T-080: Session Resume / Replay
docs.text:
• Goal: Restore session state on TUI restart. On launch, call SessionManager.loadLastCheckpoint() → if found, re-initialize: session context from checkpoint, reseed prompt from last compaction snapshot, reload token counter from checkpoint metadata, repopulate kanban from TASKS.md, and replay recent thought chains from neuralgentics. The TUI picks up exactly where it left off.
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — extend resume() to be the main entry point: call loadLastCheckpoint(), call restoreFromCheckpoint(), restore OpenCode session with reseed prompt from checkpoint, apply token counter state (delegates to T-084's restore hook), apply model preference (delegates to T-082's restore hook), trigger kanban re-parse from TASKS.md, and load recent thought chains into ChainPanel.
  • packages/tui/src/index.ts — at TUI startup, after initializing all services, call sessionManager.resume() instead of sessionManager.createSession() when a checkpoint exists. Add a startup banner: "Resuming session SESS-xxx from checkpoint at 2026-06-05 14:30 — 3 cards in progress, 2 ready."
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — extend /resume command: if checkpoint exists, report checkpoint age + state summary. If no checkpoint, suggest /compact to create one.
  • packages/tui/src/panels/chain.ts — add loadFromChain(chainId: string): Promise<void> that fetches a full thought chain from neuralgentics and populates the panel. Used on resume to restore the active chain (if any).
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/session-resume.test.ts — tests for resume flow: with checkpoint, without checkpoint, partial restore (checkpoint exists but some data missing), reseed prompt generation from checkpoint.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Token counter restore (→ T-084's hook is called; T-080 just orchestrates)
  • Model preference restore (→ T-082's hook is called; T-080 just orchestrates)
  • Opportunity cache restore (→ T-085's hook is called; T-080 just orchestrates)
  • Offline mode fallback during resume (→ T-081 — if backend is down at startup, T-080 yields to T-081)
  • HANDOFF.md auto-generation (→ P3)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — extend with resume() orchestrator: load checkpoint → restore session → restore sub-state (token, model, opportunity) → re-parse kanban → load chains → return resume summary (~120 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/index.ts — wire resume() at startup: check for checkpoint, call resume if found, show startup banner (~30 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — extend /resume to report checkpoint age + state (~20 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/panels/chain.ts — add loadFromChain(chainId) method that fetches thought chain via NeuralgenticsClient and calls addThought() for each entry (~40 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/session-resume.test.ts — 10 tests (resume with checkpoint, resume without, restore session context, reseed prompt from checkpoint, chain replay, partial restore, corrupted checkpoint skip, kanban re-parse after resume, startup banner, double-resume guard)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • TUI startup with checkpoint → loads checkpoint data and shows "Resuming session SESS-xxx at [timestamp]"
  • TUI startup without checkpoint → normal fresh session (no error)
  • Reseeds from checkpoint's facts — extracted facts are present in the restored session context
  • Recent thought chain replayed in ChainPanel within 2 seconds of startup
  • Kanban board reflects TASKS.md state (no stale data from pre-restart)
  • /resume reports checkpoint age ("Last checkpoint: 2026-06-05 14:30 — 2.3 hours ago")
  • Double-resume: calling resume() when already restored is a no-op (logs warning)
  • bun test: 10 new tests pass; 0 regressions
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • All 4 Go modules green
  • Dependencies: T-079 (checkpoint persistence — needs loadLastCheckpoint() and restoreFromCheckpoint() to exist). T-082/T-084/T-085 (restore hooks — T-080 can call stubs during development).
  • Effort: 1.0 day
  • Parallelization: Must run after T-079. Can run in parallel with T-081, T-082, T-084, T-085 (different files). Coder-only — the orchestration logic is straightforward; architect not needed.
  • Risk: MEDIUM — avoid double-initializing sidecar/SDK on resume restart. The TUI already has degraded mode in OpenCodeClient; resume must check that the client is alive before trying to restore. Mitigation: resume() checks opencodeClient.status before attempting session restore; if offline, yields to T-081's offline mode.

docs.title: Task T-081: Offline Mode (Read-Only / TUI-Only)
docs.text:
• Goal: Make the TUI functional with reduced capabilities when the OpenCode server OR Go backend is unavailable. In offline mode, the TUI provides: TASKS.md browser (read/edit cards locally), memory browser (read from saved checkpoints — no writes), ChainPanel (replays cached chains only), SpendPanel (shows last-known values from checkpoint), and OpportunityPanel (shows cached findings — no new detections). Chat panel shows "🟧 OFFLINE — Backend unavailable. Local operations only." The TUI is NEVER a dead terminal — it degrades gracefully.
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/opencode-client/client.ts — enhance degraded mode detection: if status === "offline" or status === "degraded", emit offlineState event with reason ("OpenCode server unreachable", "Go backend crashed", "both"). Add getOfflineState(): OfflineState getter.
  • packages/tui/src/neuralgentics-client/client.ts — enhance offline detection: if backendProcess === null or waitForReady() times out, set status: "offline". Emit event. Add getOfflineState().
  • packages/tui/src/index.ts — add OfflineBanner overlay component: renders "🟧 OFFLINE" banner at top of screen with reason. Wire into main render loop — check offlineState on every render tick, show/hide banner. Block all write operations (prompt send, memory.add, dispatch) when offline. Enable read operations (memory.query, kanban parse, chain replay, spend read, opportunity read).
  • packages/tui/src/panels/status.ts — extend StatusBarData with offlineState: OfflineState. Show "LLM:offline" or "Backend:offline" in status bar.
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — add /offline command: shows offline state details (what's down, what's available, last checkpoint age). Gate write commands: /compact, /scaffold (write), /resume (write) → blocked with "Unavailable in offline mode" message.
  • packages/tui/src/sidecar.ts — add checkSidecarHealth(): Promise<boolean> for offline mode diagnostics. Report in /offline.
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/offline-mode.test.ts — tests for offline detection, banner rendering, command gating, graceful degradation.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Auto-reconnection logic (retry-with-backoff on backend recovery) — P3 hardening
  • Offline memory editing (TASKS.md is editable in offline mode because it's a file; neuralgentics memories are read-only because the backend is the write path)
  • Offline opportunity detection (→ T-085 provides CACHED results; new detection requires the backend/LLM)
  • Offline dispatch or sub-agent spawning (obviously requires OpenCode)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/opencode-client/client.ts — enhance OpenCodeClient with offlineState event emitter, getOfflineState() getter, reason propagation (~40 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/neuralgentics-client/client.ts — enhance NeuralgenticsClient with offlineState, health check retry loop, event emitter (~50 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/index.ts — add OfflineBanner component (absolute-positioned text overlay), wire into render loop, gate write operations, route reads to cached data (~80 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/panels/status.ts — add offlineState to StatusBarData, render "LLM:offline"/"Backend:offline" (~15 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — add /offline command handler: display offline state details, gate write commands (~50 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/sidecar.ts — add checkSidecarHealth() (~30 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/offline-mode.test.ts — 15 tests (offline detection from OpenCodeClient, offline from NeuralgenticsClient, banner rendering, command gating for /compact, /scaffold, /resume, read operations allowed, /offline command output, sidecar health check, double-offline state, recovery detection, state transitions)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • Go backend process exits → TUI detects within 5 seconds → status bar shows "Backend:offline"
  • OpenCode server port unreachable → TUI detects within 5 seconds → status bar shows "LLM:offline"
  • Both down → banner "🟧 OFFLINE — Backend + LLM unavailable. Local operations only."
  • Offline TUI can read TASKS.md (kanban panel works), browse saved memories (from checkpoints), and view cached chain/spend/opportunity data
  • Offline TUI blocks: prompt send ("Cannot send prompts offline"), /compact ("Compaction requires backend"), /scaffold ("Cannot create cards offline"), /resume ("Resume requires a running backend")
  • /offline shows diagnostic info: what's down, what's available, last checkpoint age, sidecar status
  • Backend recovers while TUI is offline → status bar updates back to "LLM:online", banner dismissed, write operations re-enabled
  • No crash, no hang, no zombie process when entering/exiting offline mode
  • bun test: 15 new tests pass; 0 regressions
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • Dependencies: T-079 (checkpoint persistence — offline mode reads from saved checkpoints). T-080 (session resume) not strictly required but helpful for testing the end-to-end degraded flow. Can develop offline mode by stubbing checkpoint data.
  • Effort: 1.5 days
  • Parallelization: Must run after T-079. Can run in parallel with T-080, T-082, T-084, T-085 (different files). Coder-only — the state machine for offline/online transitions is straightforward; architect not needed for the implementation, though an architect sanity-check on the offline detection logic before implementation would reduce risk.
  • Risk: MEDIUM — needs careful "is the backend dead?" detection. The current degraded mode sets a flag; offline mode needs to handle BOTH services being down and distinguish which one (for diagnostics). Mitigation: health check loop runs every 5 seconds; if both health checks fail, enter full offline. If one recovers, check if the other is still needed for the user's current operation.

docs.title: Task T-082: Model-Selection Persistence
docs.text:
• Goal: Persist the active model choice as a type: "session_model_pref" memory in neuralgentics. On resume, restore the model preference from the last session instead of defaulting to the model registry's task-based routing. Add a /model slash command to view and change the current model preference. This makes the sticky per-session model choice layer described in the P2 theme.
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/agents/model-registry.ts — extend ModelRegistry with getActiveModel(): ModelConfig (returns the active model), setActiveModel(modelName: string): void (sets the active model, persists via checkpoint writer hook), restoreModelPref(modelName: string): void (restores from T-079 checkpoint or dedicated memory). Add getModelPref(): Promise<string | null> that queries neuralgentics for the most recent type: "session_model_pref" memory. Add saveModelPref(modelName: string): Promise<void> that writes a type: "session_model_pref" memory.
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — add /model handler: /model (shows current model + available models), /model <name> (sets model preference), /model reset (resets to task-based routing default). Integrate with modelRegistry.setActiveModel().
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — in restoreFromCheckpoint(), call modelRegistry.restoreModelPref() if checkpoint metadata includes a model name.
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/model-prefs.test.ts — tests for set/get/restore model preference, persistence, /model command parsing.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Dynamic mid-task model switching (v0.2.0)
  • Per-card model override (v0.2.0)
  • Cost-aware model routing (beyond scope of P2)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/agents/model-registry.ts — add getActiveModel(), setActiveModel(), restoreModelPref(), getModelPref(), saveModelPref() (~70 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — add /model command handler with view/set/reset sub-commands (~40 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — call restoreModelPref() during restoreFromCheckpoint() (~5 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/neuralgentics-client/client.ts — verify/ensure memory.query can filter on sourceType: "session_model_pref" (investigate, add ~10 lines if needed)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/model-prefs.test.ts — 8 tests (set active model, get active, restore from checkpoint metadata, persist to neuralgentics, read back, /model show, /model <name> set, /model reset)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • modelRegistry.setActiveModel("kimi-k2.6") → persists as type: "session_model_pref" memory
  • TUI restart → modelRegistry.restoreModelPref() loads "kimi-k2.6" from neuralgentics
  • /model → shows current model + list of available models from .opencode/opencode.json
  • /model deepseek-v4-pro → sets active model to deepseek-v4-pro
  • /model reset → clears preference, reverts to task-based routing
  • No model preference stored → defaults to task-based routing from registry (no error)
  • Model name not in provider list → warning "Model 'nonexistent' not found in provider registry. Falling back to default."
  • bun test: 8 new tests pass; 0 regressions
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • Dependencies: T-079 (checkpoint persistence — restore from checkpoint includes model pref), T-025 (model registry — extends it). Both DONE.
  • Effort: 0.5 day
  • Parallelization: Must run after T-079 (uses checkpoint restore hook). Can run in parallel with T-080, T-081, T-084, T-085 (different files). Coder-only — extending an existing registry with persistence hooks.
  • Risk: LOW — small, self-contained change to model registry + one new command.

docs.title: Task T-083: 3-Way Merge Viewer
docs.text:
• Goal: Extend the 2-way diff panel (panels/diff.ts, 710 lines) to support 3-way merge: base (original), ours (local), theirs (coder's proposed changes). Render three side-by-side panes in a split layout. Add keybindings: Tab to cycle active pane, 1/2/3 to jump directly, y/n to accept/reject. This upgrades the T-030 simple diff panel to a proper merge-conflict resolution tool.
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/panels/diff.ts — extend DiffPanel with renderThreeWay(base: string, ours: string, theirs: string): void. The 3-way mode uses a horizontal split: left pane (base, read-only, dimmed), center pane (ours, active by default, highlighted), right pane (theirs, selectable, highlighted). State machine: activePane: 'ours' | 'theirs' | 'base', resolved: boolean. On y accept: resolve with ours or theirs depending on active pane. On n reject: discard theirs, keep ours. On Tab: cycle ours → theirs → base → ours. On 1/2/3: jump directly to base/ours/theirs.
  • packages/tui/src/panels/diff.ts — add DiffMode enum (twoWay, threeWay). Extend renderDiffPanel() to accept mode parameter and render accordingly. The 2-way mode is unchanged; 3-way is additive.
  • packages/tui/src/index.ts — wire showDiffPanel to pass a mode parameter from the triggering command (2-way from /diff, 3-way from future merge conflict detection or /diff --threeway).
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — extend /diff to support /diff --threeway base ours theirs or /diff --merge (detect merge state automatically). When merge state detected, call renderThreeWay().
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/three-way-merge.test.ts — tests for 3-way layout, pane navigation, accept/reject with active pane selection, empty base, conflict markers, edge cases.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Automatic merge conflict detection (detecting actual git conflicts in working tree) — P3
  • Inline editing of the merge result (read-only viewer for P2; edit is v0.2.0)
  • 3-way merge for files other than code diffs (only code for now)
  • Integration with git merge (purely a viewer — the git merge happens outside the TUI)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/panels/diff.ts — add DiffMode enum, ThreeWayState interface, renderThreeWay() method, extend renderDiffPanel() with mode dispatch, add pane navigation (Tab/½/3 keybindings), add accept/reject logic with active pane awareness (~250 lines add to existing 710L)
  • packages/tui/src/commands.ts — extend /diff parser with --threeway flag + args (~30 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/index.ts — wire mode parameter into showDiffPanel (~15 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/three-way-merge.test.ts — 12 tests (3-way layout renders, pane navigation Tab cycle, pane jump ½/3, accept ours, accept theirs, reject theirs, base pane is read-only, empty base edge case, large diff rendering, single-line change, conflict markers handling, revert to 2-way mode)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • renderThreeWay(base, ours, theirs) renders three side-by-side panes with correct content
  • Tab cycles pane highlight: ours → theirs → base → ours
  • 1 jumps to base pane (read-only), 2 to ours, 3 to theirs
  • Active pane on theirs + y → accept theirs (resolve merge with their version)
  • Active pane on ours + y → accept ours (resolve merge with our version)
  • n → reject theirs entirely, keep ours
  • Base pane is visually dimmed and cannot accept/reject (read-only information only)
  • Existing 2-way /diff still works — no regression on T-030's panel
  • bun test: 12 new tests + all existing diff tests pass; 0 regressions
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • Dependencies: T-030 (diff panel) — DONE. T-021 (TUI scaffold) — DONE. No P2 dependencies.
  • Effort: 1.5 days
  • Parallelization: Fully independent — can run in parallel with T-079 in Batch 1 (zero shared files). Architect sub-call RECOMMENDED before coder implementation — the 710-line diff panel is complex and extending to 3-way needs a designed layout state machine. Architect should produce a design doc (local-only, ~100 lines) specifying: pane layout geometry, state machine transitions, keybinding routing, and how the 2-way/3-way mode dispatch works. Expected architect effort: 0.25 day. Then coder implements.
  • Risk: HIGH (design debt) — the diff panel is 710 lines with a state machine (DiffState), modal rendering, and confidence gates. Extending to 3-way risks breaking the existing 2-way flow. Mitigation: architect produces a state machine diagram + layout spec FIRST. Coder implements as an additive extension (not a rewrite).

docs.title: Task T-084: Spend History & Persistence
docs.text:
• Goal: Persist token counter state across sessions. On session end (or on manual /spend save), serialize the current TokenCounter state (per-agent, per-model, per-card breakdowns, session total) as a type: "token_ledger_batch" memory in neuralgentics. On resume, restore from the last session's batch so the SpendPanel starts with correct cumulative values instead of zero. Add /spend history subcommand to view previous sessions' spend.
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/observability/token-counter.ts — extend TokenCounter with saveBatch(): Promise<void> (serializes current state to JSON, calls neuralgenticsClient.memory.add() with type: "token_ledger_batch"). Add restoreBatch(): Promise<boolean> (queries neuralgentics for most recent batch, deserializes and restores internal state). Add getHistory(limit: number): Promise<TokenLedgerBatch[]> (queries multiple batches for history view).
  • packages/tui/src/observability/token-counter.ts — add handleSpendHistory(limit: number): CommandResult for /spend history subcommand. Shows table of previous sessions with tokens, models used, timestamp.
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — in restoreFromCheckpoint(), call tokenCounter.restoreBatch(). In session shutdown sequence, call tokenCounter.saveBatch().
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/spend-persistence.test.ts — tests for save, restore, history, edge cases.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Cross-session trend analysis or graphs (v0.2.0)
  • Budget projection across multiple sessions (v0.2.0)
  • Export to CSV or other formats (out of scope)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/observability/token-counter.ts — extend with saveBatch(), restoreBatch(), getHistory() (~60 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/observability/token-counter.ts — extend handleSpendCommand() with /spend history branch (~30 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — call restoreBatch() during restoreFromCheckpoint(), call saveBatch() on session shutdown (~10 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/spend-persistence.test.ts — 8 tests (save batch, restore batch, restore with no batch, getHistory returns correct order, session total restored, per-model breakdown restored, /spend history command output, batch timestamp accuracy)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • Session ends → TokenCounter.saveBatch() writes a type: "token_ledger_batch" memory with session total, per-model breakdown, per-agent breakdown, per-card breakdown, timestamp, session ID
  • TUI restart → restoreBatch() reads the most recent batch and populates the counter
  • SpendPanel shows correct cumulative values after restore (not zero)
  • /spend history → shows previous 5 sessions with tokens, model used, timestamp
  • No previous batch → restoreBatch() returns false; counter starts at zero gracefully
  • Batch does NOT duplicate current-session data — each session gets one batch stored once
  • bun test: 8 new tests pass; 0 regressions
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • Dependencies: T-079 (checkpoint persistence — uses restore hook in session manager), T-033 (token accountant — extends it). Both DONE.
  • Effort: 0.5 day
  • Parallelization: Must run after T-079. Can run in parallel with T-080, T-081, T-082, T-085 (different files). Coder-only — extending existing TokenCounter with serialize/deserialize.
  • Risk: LOW — small, self-contained persistence wrapper around existing counter.

docs.title: Task T-085: Offline Opportunity Cache
docs.text:
• Goal: Persist opportunity detector findings as type: "opportunity_cache" memories in neuralgentics. On resume (or in offline mode), reload previously detected candidates so the TUI can show cached opportunities without running detection (which requires the backend + LLM). Add /opportunities cached subcommand for offline browsing of previous findings.
  • Scope IN:
  • packages/tui/src/opportunity-detector/detector.ts — extend OpportunityDetector with saveCache(): Promise<void> (serializes current ranked candidates to JSON, calls neuralgenticsClient.memory.add() with type: "opportunity_cache"). Add restoreCache(): Promise<CachedCandidate[]> (queries neuralgentics for most recent cache, deserializes). Add getCachedCandidates(): CachedCandidate[] getter for the TUI panel.
  • packages/tui/src/opportunity-detector/patterns.ts — extend each detect* method to include a timestamp and sessionId in the Candidate struct (for cache dedup across sessions).
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — in restoreFromCheckpoint(), call opportunityDetector.restoreCache(). In session shutdown, call saveCache().
  • packages/tui/src/opportunity-detector/index.ts — extend handleOpportunitiesCommand() with cached subcommand. In offline mode, auto-route /opportunities to cached results (no new detection possible without backend/LLM).
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/opportunity-persistence.test.ts — tests for save, restore, offline browsing, dedup.
  • Scope OUT:
  • Running detection in offline mode (requires LLM + backend — impossible)
  • Re-triggering detection automatically from cache (cache is read-only reference)
  • Cache invalidation strategy (all cached entries expire after 7 days — simple policy)
  • Cross-session trend analysis of detected patterns (v0.2.0)
  • Sub-tasks:
  • packages/tui/src/opportunity-detector/detector.ts — extend with saveCache(), restoreCache(), getCachedCandidates() (~50 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/opportunity-detector/patterns.ts — add timestamp and sessionId to Candidate struct; populate in each detect* method (~20 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/session/session-manager.ts — call restoreCache() and saveCache() (~5 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/opportunity-detector/index.ts — add /opportunities cached subcommand handler; offline auto-routing (~30 lines add)
  • packages/tui/src/__tests__/opportunity-persistence.test.ts — 8 tests (save cache, restore cache, restore empty, offline browse, /opportunities cached command, cache dedup across sessions, 7-day expiry, timestamp accuracy)
  • Acceptance criteria:
  • Session shutdown → saveCache() writes a type: "opportunity_cache" memory with serialized RankedCandidate[]
  • TUI restart → restoreCache() loads previous candidates into the detector
  • /opportunities cached → shows last session's detected patterns with "Cached from [timestamp]" label
  • Offline mode → /opportunities auto-routes to cached results (no LLM call attempted)
  • No cached data → "No cached opportunities available. Run /opportunities when online to generate."
  • Cache entries older than 7 days → labeled "⚠️ Stale (8 days old)" — still shown but flagged
  • bun test: 8 new tests pass; 0 regressions
  • bun run typecheck: 0 errors
  • Dependencies: T-079 (checkpoint persistence — uses restore hook), T-034 (opportunity detector — extends it). Both DONE.
  • Effort: 0.5 day
  • Parallelization: Must run after T-079. Can run in parallel with T-080, T-081, T-082, T-084 (different files). Coder-only — extending existing detector with serialize/deserialize.
  • Risk: LOW — small, self-contained persistence wrapper. Cache invalidation is simple (7-day expiry; no complex policies).

docs.title: Risk Register
docs.text: Risk Likelihood Impact Mitigation T-083 (3-way merge): extending 710-line diff panel breaks 2-way mode Medium High Architect pre-designs the layout state machine (~0.25d). Coder implements as additive extension (new methods, not rewrite). 2-way regression tests must all pass before merge. T-081 (offline): detection logic falsely reports "offline" on transient blips Medium Medium Health check loop runs every 5 seconds with a 2-miss threshold (need 2 consecutive failures before entering offline). One-off blips ignored. /offline shows diagnostic detail for debugging false triggers. T-080 (resume): double-initialization of sidecar/SDK on restart Low High resume() checks opencodeClient.status before session restore. If status is "offline", yields to T-081 instead of trying to restore. Sidecar already has idempotent spawn logic (T-022). Checkpoint writes fail silently (neuralgentics backend errors) Low Medium writeCheckpoint() must throw on write failure; the session manager logs the error. Checkpoint is best-effort — missing checkpoint degrades gracefully to fresh session. Offline mode blocks too aggressively (write operations that COULD work locally are blocked) Low Low TASKS.md editing is always allowed (file operation). Only neuralgentics-backed writes are blocked. /offline clearly lists what's available.

docs.text:
Card Files Touched T-079 Checkpoint compaction/writer.ts, compaction/types.ts, compaction/orchestrator.ts, session/session-manager.ts, neuralgentics-client/client.ts (verify query support), __tests__/checkpoint-persistence.test.ts T-080 Resume session/session-manager.ts, index.ts, commands.ts, panels/chain.ts, __tests__/session-resume.test.ts T-081 Offline opencode-client/client.ts, neuralgentics-client/client.ts, index.ts, panels/status.ts, commands.ts, sidecar.ts, __tests__/offline-mode.test.ts T-082 Model Prefs agents/model-registry.ts, commands.ts, session/session-manager.ts, neuralgentics-client/client.ts (verify), __tests__/model-prefs.test.ts T-083 3-Way Merge panels/diff.ts (major extension), commands.ts, index.ts, __tests__/three-way-merge.test.ts T-084 Spend History observability/token-counter.ts, session/session-manager.ts, __tests__/spend-persistence.test.ts T-085 Offline Cache opportunity-detector/detector.ts, opportunity-detector/patterns.ts, opportunity-detector/index.ts, session/session-manager.ts, __tests__/opportunity-persistence.test.ts

Total files: ~30 source + 7 test = 37 files touched across all 7 cards. ~15 files are shared across cards; ~22 are unique.

Shared files (need careful sequencing): - session/session-manager.ts — touched by T-079, T-080, T-082, T-084, T-085 (5 cards, all add restore hooks) - commands.ts — touched by T-080, T-081, T-082, T-083 (4 cards, different command handlers) - index.ts — touched by T-080, T-081, T-083 (3 cards) - neuralgentics-client/client.ts — touched by T-079, T-081, T-082 (3 cards, different methods)
